name: Iraq
summary: Day 84 of Hormuz blockade (Friday May 22). **IRAQ JOINT PROBE OF SAUDI+UAE DRONE STRIKES (BLOOMBERG MAY 21 PRIMARY) — THE OPERATIONAL DIPLOMATIC DELIVERABLE FROM THE MAY 17 ATTACK INCIDENT. HELSINKI-PACT ARCHITECTURE ACTIVATED THROUGH INVESTIGATIVE/DIPLOMATIC CHANNEL.** Bloomberg reported May 21 that Iraq is set to jointly probe drone strikes on UAE and Saudi Arabia. FDD's Long War Journal (May 20) confirms Saudi Arabia and UAE jointly attribute the strikes to Iraqi-origin drones (consistent with al-Maliki's May 17 statement). **Saudi formal kinetic response trigger remains `watching` Day 6 of 14** — diplomatic-channel option dominant; joint probe is the operationally significant non-kinetic deliverable. **Iraq said it did not detect drones launched from its territory** (Shafaq News May 17), consistent with proxy-deniability architecture. Iran has neither acknowledged nor denied. **The Iraq joint probe operationalizes the May 19 brief's 'Saudi Day 3 of 14 — Helsinki-pact architecture prevailing' framing** at three-days-later validation point. **The investigative track preserves both Saudi mediator-credibility and UAE Barakah-mediation-space** — channels response through diplomatic architecture rather than kinetic retaliation. **The diplomatic and kinetic tracks running in parallel pattern (Sadjadpour two hubris frame second-order)**: same week Pakistan-mediated draft text leaks via Al Arabiya, IRGC threatens 'global offensive' (RHETORIC), but the regional Gulf-state architecture moves to investigative restraint. **Operational read**: Iran-backed Iraqi militia drone-harassment of GCC territory remains within deniable-harassment ceiling; no escalation to declared confrontation. PRIOR (May 19): **B1 NEW DRONE LAUNCH VECTOR — 3 DRONES LAUNCHED FROM IRAQI AIRSPACE INTERCEPTED OVER SAUDI AIRSPACE SUNDAY MAY 17 MORNING (CONFIRMED — Saudi defense ministry spokesman Brigadier Turki al-Maliki primary statement; UAE MoFA condemnation May 18).** First confirmed Saudi territorial breach of the cycle. **Iraqi-airspace origin points to Iran-backed Iraqi militia involvement** (PMU, Kata'ib Hezbollah, Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq most likely vectors by capability and prior pattern — same militias named in WSJ April 21 framing attributing approximately half of the ~1,000 drone attacks on Saudi Arabia during the war to Iran-backed Iraqi militias). **No group has publicly claimed responsibility through Monday morning IST**; non-claim itself a signal — operationally consistent with deniable harassment, not declared escalation. **Iraqi government statements through Monday morning IST do not claim or disclaim responsibility**; Iran-aligned Iraqi militia public channels (Telegram, Sabereen News) had not posted claim through search window. Saudi MoD 'reserves the right to respond at the appropriate time and place' = deliberately ambiguous holding position; new graph trigger 'Saudi formal kinetic response to May 17 Iraqi-airspace drone launch within 14 days (by 2026-05-31)' added at Day 0 of 14. **New edge added: iraq → saudi-arabia via Iran-aligned-militia proxy channel (2nd-order directness, initial weight 6.0).** **PRIOR (May 10): A10 IRGC STRIKE ON PDKI GIRDE CHAL KURDISH CAMP NORTH OF ERBIL (REPORTED) — SECOND KURDISH-CAMP STRIKE IN 5 DAYS AFTER MAY 5 KOMALA SOURDASH STRIKE.** Per FDD's Long War Journal and Hengaw monitoring, the IRGC struck the Kurdistan Democratic Party of Iran (PDKI) camp at Girde Chal (Grdachal) north of Erbil with two drones — the camp serves as a residence for PDKI member families. PDKI has now reported being attacked 114 times since the war began (May 1 figure). The Kurdistan Region was struck by 809 attacks between Feb 28 and April 20. **Structural read:** these strikes target Iranian opposition groups inside Iraq and are tactically distinct from kinetic exchanges with US forces; they reflect Iran's campaign to degrade exile opposition during the war's diplomatic phase. They do NOT constitute escalation against US/Israeli targets but they DO confirm that IRGC offensive air operations remain unimpeded by US enforcement of any 'no offensive ops' implicit MOU provision. Iraqi government typically issues protest statements (none new today). PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): Iraqi southern oil exports remain at ~900K bpd vs pre-war 3.3M bpd; force-majeure restart contingent on Pakistan-Oman-Russia track yielding or US blockade lifting; WSJ Iraq-militia attribution architecture (Saudi MoFA Apr 12 protest of Iraqi ambassador, named militias Kataib Hezbollah / Asaib Ahl al-Haq) persists. — APRIL 12 SAUDI MoFA PROTEST SUMMONS CONFIRMED, QAANI-BAGHDAD VISIT REPORTED, NAMED MILITIAS KATAIB HEZBOLLAH/ASAIB AHL AL-HAQ. WSJ April 21 framing (relayed via ISW) attributing approximately half of the ~1,000 drone attacks on Saudi Arabia during the war to Iran-backed Iraqi militias remains REPORTED single-WSJ-source pending Saudi MoD or CENTCOM independent confirmation. Operational architecture: (1) April 12 Saudi MoFA formal protest summons of Iraq's ambassador CONFIRMED via House of Saud + Defense News — warning Saudi Arabia 'would take necessary measures to ensure its national security and territorial integrity'; (2) IRGC Quds Force commander Esmail Qaani reportedly visited Baghdad during escalation period — REPORTED single-source via WSJ, indicates direct Tehran-Baghdad militia command coordination; (3) Named militias Kataib Hezbollah and Asaib Ahl al-Haq (post-2003 US-invasion origin) targeting Yanbu oil hub, Saudi Eastern Province oil installations, Kuwait airport, Bahrain sites. UNN relays as 'covert war' framing. Iraqi government silence on Saudi protest summons holds Day 60. Hormuz closure persists — Iraqi southern oil exports remain at ~900K bpd vs pre-war 3.3M bpd; April 23 ISDB shows 5 vessels all dark via Northern Corridor (88% suppression). Trump cancellation of Witkoff-Kushner dispatch (Saturday EDT) removes near-term Iraq-restart contingency; Trump 3-5 day patience window expired Sunday April 26 23:59 IST without strike — Iraq-restart sequencing now contingent on either Pakistan-Oman-Russia mediation track yielding or US blockade lifting. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]

current.restart_potential: 1.5-2.0M bpd over 2-4 weeks if FM lifted

recent_signals.headline: **SAUDI FORMAL KINETIC RESPONSE TO IRAQI-AIRSPACE DRONES — DAY 3 OF 14 NEGATIVE-SPACE; NO IRAQI GOVERNMENT PUBLIC CLAIM OR DISCLAIMER ON DRONE LAUNCHES.** Iraqi government has not surfaced public attribution either claiming or disclaiming; PMU / Kata'ib Hezbollah / Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq individually no claim. Iran has not acknowledged. The Helsinki-pact architecture (Day 5 of 30, draft expected by 2026-06-15) calls for Saudi-Iran communicative restraint — Saudi non-action over 72 hours is consistent with the Helsinki framework PREVAILING over the 'Saudi territorial breach requires Saudi response' framework. **Saudi was also one of the three Gulf leaders requesting Trump postpone the May 19 strike**, positioning Saudi firmly on the de-escalation side. Yemen's Hadi/PLC-aligned government condemned the drone attack and affirmed 'full solidarity with Riyadh' (YemenMonitor) — cleanest Hadi-government solidarity statement of the cycle, useful as confirmation of the Iraq-militia / Iran-aligned attribution path.
recent_signals.verification: Iraqi government attribution silence = CONFIRMED ABSENCE; Yemen Hadi solidarity statement = CONFIRMED (YemenMonitor); Saudi non-action Day 3 = CONFIRMED ABSENCE; al-Maliki 'right to respond' framing = RHETORIC ongoing.

recent_signals.headline: **B1 NEW DRONE LAUNCH VECTOR — 3 DRONES LAUNCHED FROM IRAQI AIRSPACE INTERCEPTED OVER SAUDI AIRSPACE SUNDAY MAY 17 MORNING.** Saudi defense ministry spokesman Brigadier Turki al-Maliki confirmed (CONFIRMED ACTION — Saudi MoD primary; UAE MoFA formal May 18 condemnation; Saudi Gazette, Times of Israel, Asharq Al-Awsat, Middle East Eye independent reporting). First confirmed Saudi territorial breach of cycle. **Iraqi-airspace origin points to Iran-backed Iraqi militia involvement (PMU, Kata'ib Hezbollah, Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq most likely vectors by capability and prior pattern — same militias named in WSJ April 21 framing).** No group publicly claimed responsibility through Monday morning IST. Iraqi government has not claimed or disclaimed; Iran-aligned militia public channels (Telegram, Sabereen News) had not posted claim. **The non-claim is itself a signal — operationally consistent with deniable harassment, not declared escalation.** Saudi MoD: 'reserves the right to respond at the appropriate time and place' = ambiguous holding position consistent with Helsinki pact preservation. **New trigger added**: 'Saudi formal kinetic response to May 17 Iraqi-airspace drone launch within 14 days (by 2026-05-31)' at Day 0 of 14. **New edge added**: iraq → saudi-arabia via Iran-aligned-militia proxy channel (2nd-order directness, initial weight 6.0). **Markets cascade**: Brent +2.0% to $111.47 (StanChart $130-145 path activates); USD/INR ₹96.29 record; Sensex/Nifty -1.30%/-1.04%. The Saudi territorial breach is one of the principal drivers per markets dossier C1 chain. **Iraqi southern oil exports remain at force-majeure ~900K bpd vs pre-war 3.3M bpd** — restart contingency unchanged.
sources: Saudi Gazette 'Al-Maliki: Three drones from Iraq intercepted' (Saudi MoD primary)
Times of Israel May 17 liveblog primary
WSJ April 21 (prior militia-vector attribution carries forward)
Markets dossier 2026-05-18 Section E (iraq-airspace-launch-vector → saudi-arabia new edge)
recent_signals.verification: 3 drones launched from Iraqi airspace + intercepted over Saudi airspace = CONFIRMED ACTION (Saudi MoD primary + UAE MoFA condemnation + multi-outlet); Iraqi government non-statement = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (search window); militia attribution = UNCLAIMED through Monday morning IST (no group posted; REPORTED speculation only by capability-and-pattern reasoning); new edge basis = REPORTED (1st-order Saudi-territorial-breach event CONFIRMED, but proxy attribution path is REPORTED single-chain by analogy).

recent_signals.headline: IRGC STRIKE ON PDKI GIRDE CHAL KURDISH CAMP NORTH OF ERBIL (REPORTED) — SECOND KURDISH-CAMP STRIKE IN 5 DAYS AFTER MAY 5 KOMALA SOURDASH STRIKE; PDKI 114 ATTACKS SINCE WAR BEGAN; KURDISTAN REGION 809 ATTACKS FEB 28 - APRIL 20. Per FDD's Long War Journal and Hengaw monitoring, IRGC struck PDKI camp Girde Chal with two drones; camp serves as residence for PDKI member families. Tactically distinct from kinetic exchanges with US/Israeli forces — reflects Iran's campaign to degrade exile opposition during war's diplomatic phase. Confirms IRGC offensive air operations remain unimpeded by US enforcement. Iraqi government issues no new protest statement.
sources: FDD's Long War Journal (May 6 piece, ongoing coverage)
recent_signals.verification: REPORTED — FDD/Hengaw multi-source (one-sided Kurdish/diaspora frame, NOT independently corroborated by Iraqi government or US/Israel)

recent_signals.headline: DAY 60 — IRAQ FORCE MAJEURE PERSISTS, NO RESTART SEQUENCING POST TRUMP CANCELLATION

historical_summaries.summary: 2 signals. Iranian drones struck coalition military camps near Erbil and Baghdad, wounding US soldiers | Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields due to Hormuz disruption

7 signals. Force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields persists — Day 17 with no resolution | Iraqi-linked oil tanker exited Hormuz via Iran on April 5 per Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker — Iraq is in Iran's three-tier 'friendly' category | IRAQ DIRECT BENEFICIARY OF CEASEFIRE — FORCE MAJEURE PATH TO RESTART
